summary: Frontend-only re-test focused on PDF export + Aspen→Pharmaceutical rebrand. React app (https://aqua-savings-sim.preview.emergentagent.com) and standalone /standalone.html both gate on aspen2050, render Talbot logo (left) + 'DIGITAL TWIN / WERC' (center) + Download PDF + Sign out (right), and produce valid 6-page landscape A4 PDFs (~1.4–1.7 MB) named 'WERC-Water-Digital-Twin-2026-04-22.pdf'. Export overlay (data-testid='export-overlay') appears during build and disappears afterwards; button re-enables. All 5 tabs navigable; sign-out returns to gate; wrong password shows 'Invalid password. Please contact your WERC facilitator.' One rebrand leak found: visible chip on True cost tab still reads 'Source · Aspen Model v6 · Inputs'.

ui_bugs.issue: Visible UI string still references 'Aspen' — chip reads 'Source · Aspen Model v6 · Inputs'. Violates the rebrand requirement ('no Aspen mentions anywhere in the visible UI'). Located on the True cost tab next to 'Underlying tariffs & escalation (2025)'.

action_items: Replace the 'Source · Aspen Model v6 · Inputs' chip text in /app/frontend/src/App.js line 388 with a non-branded label (e.g. 'Source · Water Model v6 · Inputs') so the React UI is fully rebranded — standalone footer/text already uses 'Water Model v6'.
critical_code_review_comments: App.js is now 916 lines (was 742); still violates the >700-line guideline. Recommend extracting downloadPDF() helper and the per-tab components into separate files.

downloadPDF() in App.js mutates `tab` state mid-export to force each tab to render before html2canvas capture; if the user clicks any tab while exporting the loop will race. Consider disabling the tab bar (or wrapping in an off-screen render) while `exporting` is true.

Both downloadPDF implementations swallow per-tab capture errors silently (`if (!main) continue;`). If one tab fails the PDF is silently shorter. Suggest at minimum logging or surfacing the partial-export warning in the overlay status.

loadImage() in App.js (lines 898-911) uses crossOrigin='anonymous' against a same-origin asset; harmless but the silent catch around it (line 718-722) means a logo failure produces a coverless cover page with no diagnostic. Add a console.warn.
html2canvas captures with scale=2 and the simulator/savings tabs include large Recharts SVGs — on lower-memory clients this may OOM. Consider scale: window.devicePixelRatio or a 1.5 cap.

Both PDFs rely on a wall-clock wait (900–1400 ms) for chart animations. If Recharts animations are delayed (slow CPU) the captured chart may still be tweening. Better: disable Recharts animation during export (`isAnimationActive={false}` while `exporting`).

success_rate.frontend: 95% (all functionality works; one visible-text rebrand miss)

test_credentials: Demo password: aspen2050 (still working; gate also rejects wrong password with the expected error message)
seed_data_creation: None — used existing in-code static data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: PDF flow verified end-to-end: React PDF was 1,677,257 bytes / 6 pages; standalone PDF was 1,467,224 bytes / 6 pages. Filename pattern: WERC-Water-Digital-Twin-YYYY-MM-DD.pdf. Both expose the export overlay (React: data-testid='export-overlay'; standalone: button#pdfBtn becomes disabled). Visible-text 'Aspen' scan over all 5 React tabs returned hits ONLY on True cost tab: 'Source · Aspen Model v6 · Inputs' (App.js:388). Standalone scan returned 0 hits. CSS variables/class names like --aspen-green are not user-visible and don't count.
rca of the issue: When the app was rebranded from 'Aspen' to 'Pharmaceutical Company / WERC', the chip label inside the True cost tab tariff card was missed because the text is hardcoded in JSX (App.js:388) rather than driven by the SITE_INFO/site.name field. Fix: change the literal string to 'Source · Water Model v6 · Inputs' (matches the standalone build and the dashboard footer).
